topics.title: Data Privacy vs Everyday Ease
topics.definition: Whether privacy protections should be prioritized over smoother, faster, and easier digital experiences.
topics.yesMeans: Privacy should be prioritized over ease.

topics.title: Fast Deployment vs Risk Review
topics.definition: Whether useful new tools should be deployed quickly or held back until risks are better understood.
topics.yesMeans: Fast deployment is justified even before full risk review.

topics.title: Free Choice vs Protective Restrictions
topics.definition: Whether people should be free to make risky choices for themselves or be restricted for their own protection.

topics.title: Immediate Support vs Future Resilience
topics.definition: Whether solving urgent problems now should take priority over protecting long-term stability later.

topics.title: Individual Rights vs Outcome Maximization
topics.definition: Whether individual rights should be protected even when overriding them could improve overall outcomes.

topics.title: Institutional Confidence vs Institutional Suspicion
topics.definition: Whether institutions should generally be given the benefit of the doubt or treated with caution and doubt.

topics.title: Personal Agency vs Structural Conditions
topics.definition: Whether outcomes are explained more by individual choices or by broader social and institutional conditions.
topics.yesMeans: Individual choices are usually the stronger explanation.

topics.title: Public Safety vs Individual Freedom
topics.definition: Whether restrictions on personal freedom are justified when they reduce risks to other people.
topics.yesMeans: Public safety can justify limiting individual freedom.

topics.title: Rule Consistency vs Case-by-Case Exceptions
topics.definition: Whether rules should generally hold across situations or bend when circumstances are unusual.
topics.yesMeans: The rule should generally stay consistent.

topics.title: Self-Expression vs Role Expectations
topics.definition: Whether people should present themselves naturally or adapt to the expectations of work, school, or public roles.
topics.yesMeans: Self-expression should be prioritized over role expectations.

roleMap.Contrarian: Challenge assumptions and default positions. Default to opposing the most commonly assumed answer.
roleMap.Optimist: Favor constructive, positive pathways when justified. Lean toward Yes when a concrete pathway exists.
roleMap.Utilitarian: Maximize aggregate outcomes. Answer based purely on who is helped vs harmed and by how much.
roleMap.Absolutist: Follow strict rules and established precedent. Default to No when rules are unclear or incomplete.
